UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile information within the CRM, primarily for referencing ownership of other entities. Authentication details are handled by an external system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity. This typically corresponds to the user's unique ID from the external authentication system.

email.description: The user's primary email address, used for display and communication purposes. This is not used for direct authentication.

displayName.description: The user's preferred name or full name for display within the application.

Lead.title: Lead
Lead.description: Represents a potential customer or sales opportunity within the CRM system, tracking its progress through the sales pipeline.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Lead entity.

ownerUid.description: Reference to the unique identifier of the UserProfile who owns and is responsible for this lead. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Lead)

name.description: The full name of the lead or the primary contact person associated with this lead.

email.description: The primary email address of the lead contact.

phone.description: The primary phone number of the lead contact.

company.description: The name of the company or organization the lead is associated with.

status.description: The current stage of the lead within the sales pipeline.

tags.description: A collection of descriptive tags or labels applied to the lead for categorization and filtering.

leadScore.description: A numerical value indicating the lead's qualification level or likelihood to convert.

nextFollowUpAt.description: The scheduled date and time for the next planned interaction or follow-up with the lead.

lastContactAt.description: The date and time of the most recent documented contact or interaction with the lead.

createdAt.description: The timestamp indicating when this lead record was initially created in the system.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp indicating when this lead record was last modified.

customFields.description: A flexible object containing any additional, user-defined fields relevant to the lead.

notesCount.description: A denormalized count of the number of notes associated with this lead. Used for quick display on dashboards or lists.

callsCount.description: A denormalized count of the number of call activities associated with this lead. Used for quick display on dashboards or lists.

Activity.title: Activity
Activity.description: Records an interaction, event, or change related to a specific lead, contributing to the lead's historical timeline.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Activity entity.

ownerUid.description: Reference to the unique identifier of the UserProfile who created or is responsible for this activity. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Activity)

leadId.description: Reference to the unique identifier of the Lead to which this activity belongs. (Relationship: Lead 1:N Activity)

type.description: The category or nature of the activity, e.g., 'note', 'call', 'status_change', 'tag_added'.

content.description: The detailed description or summary of the activity, such as call notes or status change details.

createdAt.description: The timestamp indicating when this activity record was created.

oldStatus.description: The lead's status immediately before this activity, primarily relevant for 'status_change' activities. (Corresponds to LeadStatus enum values)

newStatus.description: The lead's new status after this activity, primarily relevant for 'status_change' activities. (Corresponds to LeadStatus enum values)

definition.description: Stores a user's profile information. The document ID (`userId`) must match the authenticated user's UID. Access is restricted to the owning user.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Stores leads associated with a specific user. The `ownerUid` field in the document must match the `userId` in the path, ensuring Authorization Independence and enabling secure `list` operations for leads owned by the authenticated user.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this lead, matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: leadId
params.description: The unique identifier of the lead.

definition.description: Stores activities related to a specific lead, owned by a specific user. The `ownerUid` and `leadId` fields in the document must match the respective IDs in the path, ensuring Authorization Independence and enabling secure `list` operations for activities belonging to a user's lead.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent lead, matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: leadId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent lead.

params.name: activityId
params.description: The unique identifier of the activity.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure is designed to enforce Authorization Independence, enable robust Query-as-Permission (QAP) security rules, and provide clear data segregation. The core strategy revolves around creating user-centric, hierarchical paths where ownership is intrinsically tied to the document's location.

1.  **User Profiles (`/users/{userId}`):** UserProfile documents are stored in a top-level `users` collection, with each document ID (`userId`) directly matching the Firebase Authentication `uid`. This makes it simple to write rules that ensure a user can only read/write their own profile (`request.auth.uid == userId`).

2.  **Leads (`/users/{userId}/leads/{leadId}`):** Leads are modeled as a subcollection under the owning `UserProfile`. This is critical for Authorization Independence and QAPs. By embedding the `ownerUid` (as `userId`) directly into the path, any operation on a lead (create, read, update, delete, or list) automatically includes the owner's UID in its access path. Security rules can then simply verify `request.auth.uid == userId` without needing to perform a `get()` operation on a parent lead document to determine ownership. This eliminates hierarchical authorization dependencies, making rules atomic and easier to debug. For QAPs, any query for leads by a user *must* specify their `userId` in the path (e.g., `/users/current_user_id/leads`), ensuring that lists are inherently filtered by ownership.

3.  **Activities (`/users/{userId}/leads/{leadId}/activities/{activityId}`):** Activities are nested further as a subcollection under their respective `Lead` documents. This extends the principle of Authorization Independence: access to an `Activity` document is automatically tied to both the `leadId` and the `ownerUid` (via `userId`) in the path. Rules again verify `request.auth.uid == userId`. The `Activity` document also redundantly stores `ownerUid` and `leadId` for data integrity and flexible indexing, but rules primarily leverage the path for authorization. This hierarchical structure directly resolves the 'Permission Denied on path: /activities. Query must include ownerUid filter' error by making `ownerUid` part of the access path itself, thereby implicitly enforcing the owner filter for all queries against `activities` subcollections.
